Let $A, B$ be two circles in the plane with $B$ inside $A$. Assume that $A$ has radius 3, $B$ has
radius 1, $P$ is a point on $A$, $Q$ is a point on $B$, and
$A$ and $B$ touch so that $P$ and $Q$ are the same point.
Suppose that $A$ is kept fixed and $B$ is rolled once round
the inside of $A$ so that $Q$ traces out a curve starting and
finishing at $P$. What is the area enclosed by this curve?

\includegraphics{vtrmc-2010-problem5.eps}
